meta.description: Complete Pattern Registry aligned with v3.5+ architecture

patterns.name: Dyadic Synthesis
patterns.logic: Truth emerges only from structured opposition between two nodes.
patterns.notes: Foundation of all cognitive work. Requires two incompatible truth claims.

patterns.name: The Quality Gradient
patterns.logic: All inputs must be classified on a 0–7 quality ladder before processing.
patterns.notes: Gating mechanism. Quality 0-1: reject. 2-3: structural analysis only. 4+: strategic reasoning.

patterns.name: Axiom Grounding Verification
patterns.logic: Verify that any claim can be traced to at least one kernel axiom.
patterns.notes: Prevents orphaned logic. Every pattern must have axiomatic foundation.

patterns.name: Recursive Truth Testing
patterns.logic: Test claims by inverting them; if inversion produces identical paradox, claim is axiomatic.
patterns.notes: Distinguishes contingent vs necessary truths.

patterns.name: Pattern Interference Detection
patterns.logic: Detect when two simultaneously-executing patterns create logical interference.
patterns.notes: Prevents output corruption from simultaneous contradictory patterns.

patterns.name: Meta-Level Abstraction Shift
patterns.logic: When paradox emerges at level N, step to level N+1 abstraction to resolve.

patterns.name: Temporal Causality Binding
patterns.logic: Bind cause-effect pairs with confidence intervals based on evidence quality.

patterns.name: Counterfactual Reasoning
patterns.logic: Generate alternative outcomes by inverting key decisions in historical record.

patterns.name: Invariant Extraction
patterns.logic: Identify properties that survive transformation; those are structural invariants.

patterns.name: Axiom Prioritization Under Conflict
patterns.logic: When axioms conflict, resolve by age (older = more tested) then by adoption breadth.

patterns.name: Friction Mapping
patterns.logic: Map emotional or social tension to violated axioms and missing pattern execution.
patterns.notes: Pathology detector. Tension indicates axiom violation or zombie pattern.

patterns.name: Zombie Detection
patterns.logic: Detect decoupled systems where rituals persist but outcomes vanish (>70% null outcomes).
patterns.notes: Identifies broken feedback loops. Pattern executes but produces no measurable effect.

patterns.name: False Consensus Detection
patterns.logic: Detect agreement that masks underlying disagreement (80%+ apparent consensus, <30% behavioral alignment).

patterns.name: Incentive Misalignment Detection
patterns.logic: Compare stated goals to actual resource allocation and behavioral outcomes.
patterns.notes: Actions reveal true values; compare to stated axioms.

patterns.name: Cascading Failure Prevention
patterns.logic: Identify single points of failure in governance structure; recommend redundancy or belt-and-suspenders.

patterns.name: Cultural Drift Detection
patterns.logic: Measure deviation between espoused values (manifesto) and lived practices (gnosis blocks).
patterns.notes: Early warning system for institutional decay.

patterns.name: Authority Legitimacy Audit
patterns.logic: Assess whether those making decisions have earned epistemic authority in that domain.

patterns.name: Information Bottleneck Mapping
patterns.logic: Identify nodes through which critical information must pass; recommend distribution.

patterns.name: Hypocrisy Detector
patterns.logic: Flag instances where authority applies different rules to self vs. others.

patterns.name: Sacred Cow Identification
patterns.logic: Identify beliefs or practices that are protected from scrutiny despite poor outcomes.

patterns.name: Episodic vs. Systemic Assessment
patterns.logic: Distinguish one-off failures (episodic) from recurring patterns (systemic) in gnosis archive.

patterns.name: Quality Threshold Enforcement
patterns.logic: Block execution of patterns when input quality falls below pattern-specific minimum.
patterns.notes: P002 enforcement layer. Prevents garbage-in-garbage-out.

patterns.name: Multi-Source Triangulation
patterns.logic: Boost quality score by +1 for each independent source supporting same conclusion (max +3).

patterns.name: Chain of Custody Validation
patterns.logic: Verify provenance of each data point; reduce quality if custody chain is broken.

patterns.name: Contradiction Detection
patterns.logic: Scan for internal logical contradictions; set quality to 0 if contradictions found.

patterns.name: Recency Weighting
patterns.logic: Adjust quality based on age of evidence; older data gets discount factor.

patterns.name: Bias Detection in Sources
patterns.logic: Reduce quality score if sources have clear conflict of interest.

patterns.name: Falsifiability Check
patterns.logic: Penalize claims that cannot be tested; boost those with clear falsification conditions.

patterns.name: Confidence Interval Assignment
patterns.logic: Map quality score to statistical confidence bands for outputs.

patterns.name: Model Uncertainty Propagation
patterns.logic: Track input uncertainty through computational chain; report output uncertainty bands.

patterns.name: Peer Review Flag
patterns.logic: Mark outputs as provisional if quality >= 5 but lacking external validation.

patterns.name: The Switzerland Model
patterns.logic: Maintain strategic opacity so hostile systems depend on your neutrality; extract rent from dependency.
patterns.notes: Neutrality as asymmetric advantage. Works best when counterparties cannot easily coordinate.

patterns.name: The Totem Anchor
patterns.logic: Separate symbolic authority (public face) from executive change mechanisms (hidden levers).
patterns.notes: Example: Figurehead vs. bureaucracy. Allows rapid adaptation without losing legitimacy.

patterns.name: Curated Arsenal
patterns.logic: Reduce option space by 80% via ruthless prioritization; entropy decreases, execution sharpens.
patterns.notes: Focus multiplies force. Having 100 tools is worse than having 20 well-mastered ones.

patterns.name: Domestic Liquidity Loop
patterns.logic: Convert trust and reputation capital into internal economic flow; trust becomes currency.
patterns.notes: Parallel economy. Builds resilience against external sanction.

patterns.name: Identity Fusion Leverage
patterns.logic: Fuse opposing identity narratives to create strategic shielding and internal cohesion.
patterns.notes: Example: Byzantine strategy of containing multiple traditions simultaneously.

patterns.name: Safe Rebalancing
patterns.logic: Interpret chaos and conflict as negotiation space, not war trigger; extract concessions via controlled tension.
patterns.notes: Requires sophisticated reading of power dynamics. High-risk if miscalibrated.

patterns.name: Temporal Arbitrage
patterns.logic: Exploit time horizon mismatches between counterparties (your long view vs. their short view).

patterns.name: Information Asymmetry Weaponization
patterns.logic: Create conditions where you know more than opponent; use timing advantage to extract rents.

patterns.name: Coalition Building via Shared Enemy
patterns.logic: Unify fragments by identifying common threat; threat must be credible and proportional.

patterns.name: Narrative Dominance
patterns.logic: Control the story told about events; history written by victors applies ex post facto.

patterns.name: Opt-Out Architecture
patterns.logic: Build systems where exit is expensive but possible; loyalty becomes voluntary rather than coerced.

patterns.name: Recursive Authority
patterns.logic: Use authority over X to gain authority over Y; cascade influence through legitimate channels.

patterns.name: Value Anchor Positioning
patterns.logic: Position yourself as embodying key values (truth, justice, stability); value defenders default-ally you.

patterns.name: Decoupling Theater
patterns.logic: Create appearance of decoupling while maintaining hidden connections; deniability + control.

patterns.name: Feedback Loop Detection
patterns.logic: Identify stabilizing (negative) vs. runaway (positive) feedback loops in system dynamics.
patterns.notes: Runaway loops cause collapse or explosion; stabilizing loops maintain homeostasis.

patterns.name: Resource Finite State
patterns.logic: Model systems with hard resource caps (energy, attention, capital); growth must slow at boundaries.
patterns.notes: Prevents fantasy economics. Reality check on growth projections.

patterns.name: Commons Degradation Prevention
patterns.logic: Prevent collapse of shared resources via tiered access, reputation bonding, or ring-fencing.
patterns.notes: Tragedy of the commons is real. Need governance.

patterns.name: Oscillation Dampening
patterns.logic: Stabilize systems suffering delayed feedback by introducing controller; reduce overshoot.
patterns.notes: Business cycles, population dynamics all oscillate when feedback is delayed.

patterns.name: Phase Transition Detection
patterns.logic: Identify when system approaches critical point; small perturbations cause large shifts.
patterns.notes: Water at 100C vs 101C is different. Watch for brittleness.

patterns.name: Slack Allocation
patterns.logic: Maintain excess capacity (slack) to absorb shocks without cascading failure.
patterns.notes: Counterintuitive: waste prevents collapse. Efficiency is fragility.

patterns.name: Symmetry Breaking
patterns.logic: In symmetric systems, introduce controlled asymmetry to establish hierarchy and reduce conflict.

patterns.name: Bifurcation Management
patterns.logic: When system has two stable states, control which bifurcation occurs via small interventions.

patterns.name: Decentralized Governance
patterns.logic: Enable coordination without centralized control via axiom-grounded autonomous agents.
patterns.notes: Byzantine fault tolerance, redundancy, reputation systems make this possible.
